task_completion.description: Open TopoCal and access the traverse computation or adjustment module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The TopoCal application window is open, and a specific dialog, tab, or window for traverse computation/adjustment is visible.

task_completion.description: Input the starting parameters for Station 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The values Easting = 500000.000, Northing = 4400000.000, and Starting Azimuth = 45°30'00" are entered into the corresponding starting parameter fields in the TopoCal interface.

task_completion.description: Input the interior angles and horizontal distances for the 5 stations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A data entry grid or form in TopoCal is populated with interior angles and horizontal distances for 5 distinct stations.

task_completion.description: Compute the closure error of the traverse.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Compute', 'Calculate', or 'Check Closure' action has been performed, and the closure error results (such as linear or angular misclosure) are displayed on the screen.

task_completion.description: Adjust the traverse using the Compass (Bowditch) rule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Compass' or 'Bowditch' rule is selected from a dropdown, checkbox, or radio button list for adjustment methods, and the adjusted coordinates are generated.

task_completion.description: Export the final adjusted Easting and Northing coordinates to the specified text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, export confirmation, or the final saved text file 'traverse_results.txt' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\' is visible, containing the adjusted Easting and Northing coordinates for all 5 stations.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent used the TopoCal graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the TopoCal application windows and menus, rather than using a terminal, Python script, or command-line tool to compute the traverse.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent actually entered the data into the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The TopoCal data grid shows the populated values from the CSV and TXT files, proving the data was loaded or typed into the application.

integrity.description: Verifies the final results file was exported from TopoCal.
integrity.visual_evidence: An export or save dialog originating from TopoCal is visible, ensuring the agent didn't just manually create 'traverse_results.txt' in a text editor and type fabricated results.
